question:
We got a NullPointerException when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT 

After logging into FileNet Workplace XT, a java.lang.NullPointerException error is logged when bootstrapping the object store or when accessing Site Preferences/Access Role.

Here's the error stack,

Error Stack Trace extracted from the J2EE Application Server error log:

java.lang.NullPointerException

at org.apache.jasper.runtime.JspWriterImpl.write(JspWriterImpl.java:421)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRoleMembers(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:254)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRole(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:220)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.renderAdminRole(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:131)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)
ground_truth: Based on the provided context, the NullPointerException occurring when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT is likely caused by an invalid entry specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). To resolve the issue, you need to change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry, such as cn, displayName or uid, in FEM and then restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances. This should prevent the NullPointerException error from occurring.
answer:
The NullPointerException error when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on Workplace XT is likely caused by an invalid entry in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute setting in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM).

To resolve this issue, you need to change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry such as cn, displayName or uid in FEM, and then restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances.
contexts:
java.lang.NullPointerException; workplacext bootstrap; user display name TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 After logging into FileNet Workplace XT, a java.lang.NullPointerException error is logged when bootstrapping the object store or when accessing Site Preferences/Access Role. 

SYMPTOM
Error Stack Trace extracted from the J2EE Application Server error log: 

java.lang.NullPointerException 

 at org.apache.jasper.runtime.JspWriterImpl.write(JspWriterImpl.java:421) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRoleMembers(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:254) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRole(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:220) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.renderAdminRole(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:131) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)


CAUSE
This behavior may occur if an invalid entry has been specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute setting in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). 

Workplace XT attempts to resolve the user names and groups defined within the Access Roles based on the Directory Service configuration defined in FEM. If the user name query resolves to null or if there is an invalid entry a NullPointerException error may occur. 


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 In FEM, change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry such as cn, displayName or uid and restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances.

question:
DFHAP1203I and DFHSR0601 with 0C1 in EQA10OSM after upgrading to CICS TS 5.2 Why is my CICS region failing during startup with message DFHAP1203I followed by DFHSR0601 after I upgraded to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V5.2? A system dump shows an abend 0C1 occurring in the Debug Tool for z/OS module EQA10OSM. 
I receive the following messages:
DFHAP1203I Language Environment is being initialized. 
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task III in control.
ground_truth: The issue is occurring due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, specifically CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA. To resolve this problem, ensure that there is only one instance of CEEPLPKA and CEECCICS in your library concatenations, and that they are at the correct z/OS level.
answer:
The issue you're experiencing after upgrading to CICS TS 5.2 is likely due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules. This can cause the Language Environment to fail initialization, resulting in the DFHAP1203I message.

The subsequent DFHSR0601 message indicates a program interrupt occurred with system task III in control. This suggests that the Language Environment initialization failed, causing the system task to interrupt the program.

The abend 0C1 in the Debug Tool for z/OS module EQA10OSM indicates that the failure occurred in this module. This module is part of the Language Environment, which is responsible for initializing the Language Environment.

To resolve this issue, ensure that there is only one instance of the Language Environment modules in your library concatenations, and that they are at the correct z/OS level. This will prevent any mismatches in z/OS levels, allowing the Language Environment to initialize correctly and preventing the system task from interrupting the program.

DFHSR0622 An attempt to overwrite the ERDSA has caused the abend which follows
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task TCP in control
DFHKE1800 ABNORMAL TERMINATION OF CICS IS COMPLETE 

SYMPTOM
DFHSR0622 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v3r2/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhsr/SR0622.html] An attempt to overwrite the ERDSA has caused the abend which follows
DFHSR0601 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v3r2/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhsr/SR0601.html] Program interrupt occurred with system task TCP in control
DFHKE1800 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v3r2/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhke/KE1800.html] ABNORMAL TERMINATION OF CICS IS COMPLETE

CAUSE
Program check in Compuware module DBUGPCPA. CICS ends since the program check occurs in a module that is running under the main CICS terminal control task.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
You examine a system dump on the DFHSR0601 [http://publibfi.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr/BOOKS/dfhg4c00/1.87?ACTION=MATCHES&REQUEST=dfhsr0601&TYPE=FUZZY&SHELF=dfhasj00&DT=20070614214024&CASE=&searchTopic=TOPIC&searchText=TEXT&searchIndex=INDEX&rank=RANK&ScrollTOP=FIRSTHIT#FIRSTHIT] message and find that the running task is TCP CSTP.

The ABEND0C4 occurs in Compuware module DBUGPCPA on a STM instruction: 90ECD00C. This is because register 13 is pointing to "read only" or key 0 storage. In this case, it is pointing to module EYU9NA01.

You back up in the CICS trace and find that the TCP task was in the middle of processing a losterm error for a terminal attached to CICS.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21272740&aid=1]Compuware [http://www.compuware.com/] has provided fix XDPF265.

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You create a System Event using the CICS Explorer and export the bundle file to your z/OS HFS UNIX® File System. During CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V4.1 startup your bundle install fails with messages DFHEC1003 reason BAD_XML_DATA and DFHRL0102E reason CALL_BACK_ERROR. 

SYMPTOM
You see the following messages in CICS job log (MSGUSR):

DFHRL0107 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhrl/RL0107I.html] I The CICS resource life-cycle manager has started to create the BUNDLE resource bundlename.

DFHPI1007 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhpi/PI1007.html] XML to data transformation failed because of incorrect input (UNDEFINED_ELEMENT fileEnableStatus) for EVENTBINDING name.evbind.

DFHEC1003 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhec/EC1003.html] The CICS event capture component failed to create the EVENTBINDING resource name.evbind reason BAD_XML_DATA.

DFHRL0102 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhrl/RL0102E.html] E The CICS resource life-cycle manager failed to create the resource name.evbind and returned with reason CALL_BACK_ERROR.


CAUSE
You have created a System Event [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.eventprocessing.doc/concepts/dfhep_systemevents.html] using the CICS Explorer version 1.1.x and you are trying to install the Event Bundle in CICS TS 4.1. CICS System Events is a new feature for CICS TS 4.2 and is not compatible with CICS TS 4.1. In this case, the example CICS FILE ENABLE STATUS event was used. Since this is a System Event, it can not be used with CICS TS V4.1.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Either upgrade to CICS TS V4.2 to use CICS System Event functions within the CICS Explorer version 1.1.x or do not create a System Event if you plan to install the bundle in a CICS TS V4.1 region.

There are several examples shipped with the CICS Explorer V1.1.x in the Explorer > New Wizards > Other > Examples folder that are System Events. Do not use these examples if you plan to use your bundle with CICS TS V4.1: 

 * CICS DB2 CONNECTION STATUS event 
 * CICS FILE ENABLE STATUS event 
 * CICS FILE OPEN STATUS event 
 * CICS TASK THRESHOLD event 
 * CICS TRANCLASS TASK THRESHOLD event 
 * CICS Unhandled TRANSACTION ABEND event


The CICS TS V4.2 information center lists the System Events [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.eventprocessing.doc/concepts/dfhep_systemevents.html] that are supported in V4.2:  * FILE enable or disable status 
 * FILE open or close status 
 * DB2CONN connection status 
 * TASK threshold 
 * TRANCLASS TASK threshold 
 * Unhandled transaction abend


A request for enhancement [https://www.ibm.com/developerworks/rfe/] (RFE) (number 16580) has been submitted for review by CICS Development to request that only functions for CICS TS V4.1 are available, without the CICS Explorer version 1.1.x System Event functions, if using CICS TS V4.1.

question:
We got a NullPointerException when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT 

After logging into FileNet Workplace XT, a java.lang.NullPointerException error is logged when bootstrapping the object store or when accessing Site Preferences/Access Role.

Here's the error stack,

Error Stack Trace extracted from the J2EE Application Server error log:

java.lang.NullPointerException

at org.apache.jasper.runtime.JspWriterImpl.write(JspWriterImpl.java:421)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRoleMembers(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:254)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRole(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:220)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.renderAdminRole(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:131)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)
ground_truth: Based on the provided context, the NullPointerException occurring when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT is likely caused by an invalid entry specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). To resolve the issue, you need to change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry, such as cn, displayName or uid, in FEM and then restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances. This should prevent the NullPointerException error from occurring.
answer:
This behavior may occur if an invalid entry has been specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute setting in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). 

Workplace XT attempts to resolve the user names and groups defined within the Access Roles based on the Directory Service configuration defined in FEM. If the user name query resolves to null or if there is an invalid entry a NullPointerException error may occur. 

To resolve this issue, you should inspect the Directory Services Configuration in FEM and ensure that the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute is set to a valid LDAP entry such as cn, displayName or uid. After making the necessary changes, restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances.

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Getting a NullPointerException from WorkplaceXT/Application Integration 

SYMPTOM
Getting prompted to logon twice when adding a document through Application Integration for WorkplaceXT/Microsoft Outlook and getting a NullPointerException when logging on at second time.

After the first logon, user was promoted to logon again. When logging on at the second time, the following error occurred:

--------------------------
"IBM FileNet Workplace XT Add: Unable to Perform Add Operation"

Operation: SelectedAddProfile
Error Name: E_SVRCMDXMLPARSERFAILED
HRESULT: 0x8004042b
Description: Unable to parse command XML:null
Details: java.lang.NullPointerException
-------------------------

After the error message dialog box was dismissed, adding a document from Outlook did not prompt for logon again for the current Outlook session. After starting a new Outlook session, the symptom repeated again.


CAUSE
Since the WorkplaceXT was not configured for Single Sign On (SSO), the first logon was expected for each Outlook session. The unexpected second logon was due to the Application Integration error (see above).

This error was caused by the jar files in <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory of the WebShpere Applicaton Server (WAS) hosting the WorkplaceXT.

In reference to the following page at P8 Knowledge Center 


"Configuring WebSphere Application Server to support user tokens and WebDAV connections for Workplace XT"

http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSNW2F_5.2.1/com.ibm.p8.installingxt.doc/wxtip018.htm [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSNW2F_5.2.1/com.ibm.p8.installingxt.doc/wxtip018.htm]

there should be only two jar files in the following directory in WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT:
<install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Steps to resolve the problem: 

1. Stopped WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT
2. Removed any additional jar files from the <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory so that the directory contained only two jar files: 

- authentication-websphere.jar
- log4j_1.2.13.jar

3. Deleted WorkplaceXT folder from the WAS wstemp folder, and clear the browser cache
4. Started WebSphere Application Server 

After implementing the above procedure, the problem was resolved. User started to get a logon prompt only once as expected.

question: Does the BPM internal Document Store work with IBM DB2 pureScale? I use IBM DB2 pureScale with my BPM installation. During startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store I see hanging threads in the systemOut.log and the process will not finish. How can this be solved?
ground_truth:
Yes, the BPM internal Document Store can work with IBM DB2 pureScale. If you are experiencing hanging threads during startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store, it may be due to issues with the pureScale setup and configuration. 

One potential solution is to ensure that the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity are correctly configured on Tomcat. This involves setting pureScale member host names in the operating system's hosts file, adding JNDI resource information to the web application environment file, adding JNDI data source information to the Tomcat configuration file, and implementing custom code to retrieve supported user attributes from the underlying security repository.

By correctly configuring the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity on Tomcat, you may be able to resolve the hanging threads issue during BPM server startup and internal document store initialization.
answer:
Based on the provided context, the issue you are experiencing with hanging threads during the startup of the IBM Business Process Manager server and initialization of the internal document store, when using IBM DB2 pureScale, is likely related to a transaction timeout.

To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

1.  Check the SystemOut.log file for the following message: `WSVR0605Wl`. This message indicates a transaction timeout.
2.  Increase the transaction timeout value. You can do this by setting the `transaction.timeout` property in the `server.xml` file to a higher value, such as `99999`.
3.  If the issue persists, you can try setting the `getAllGroups` property to `false` in the `server.xml` file. This property is related to the document store and may be causing the hanging threads.
4.  Ensure that the DB2 pureScale configuration is correct and that the database connection is stable.
5.  If none of the above steps resolve the issue, you may need to gather more information and contact IBM Support for further assistance.

It's also worth noting that the issue you are experiencing may be related to the problem described in the "An IBM Business Process Manager (BPM) server might crash due to a hung thread on a JDBC call" technote (1670914). In this case, you may need to apply the fix mentioned in the technote to resolve the issue.
contexts:
startup; start; hang; hung; getAllGroups; transaction timeout; timeout; WSVR0605Wl; 99999; LSW_USR TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 When you start IBM Business Process Manager server, you see a hung thread and a transaction timeout in log file. 

SYMPTOM
You may see the following messages in SystemOut.log file:

MustGather; BPM document store TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You are having a BPM document store problem with the IBM Business Process Manager products. For example, you might be experiencing a problem when you attach or migrate documents. You would like to know what information you must collect (MustGather) so that the IBM Business Process Manager Support team can diagnose your problem. If you gather this documentation before contacting support it will expedite the troubleshooting process, and save you time. 

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Collect troubleshooting information for BPM document store problems in IBM Business Process Manager. Gathering this information before calling IBM support helps in understanding the problem and saves time when data is analyzed.



SETTING THE TRACE STRING

Oracle ORA-01000 error caused by IBM Business Process Manager (BPM) SQL integration service (1670886) [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21670886]
Versions: 8.5, 8.0.1, 8.0, 7.5.1, 7.5
You can use IBM Business Process Manager SQL integration service to interact with the database. In a high workload, you might find that the prepared statement cache hit ratio is very low for the data source that is used by the SQL integration service. After increasing the statement cache size in the data source, an Oracle ORA-01000 error happens.



An IBM Business Process Manager (BPM) server might crash due to a hung thread on a JDBC call (1670914) [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21670914]

question: Does the BPM internal Document Store work with IBM DB2 pureScale? I use IBM DB2 pureScale with my BPM installation. During startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store I see hanging threads in the systemOut.log and the process will not finish. How can this be solved?
ground_truth:
Yes, the BPM internal Document Store can work with IBM DB2 pureScale. If you are experiencing hanging threads during startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store, it may be due to issues with the pureScale setup and configuration. 

One potential solution is to ensure that the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity are correctly configured on Tomcat. This involves setting pureScale member host names in the operating system's hosts file, adding JNDI resource information to the web application environment file, adding JNDI data source information to the Tomcat configuration file, and implementing custom code to retrieve supported user attributes from the underlying security repository.

By correctly configuring the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity on Tomcat, you may be able to resolve the hanging threads issue during BPM server startup and internal document store initialization.
answer:
To resolve the issue, follow these steps:

1. Check the DB2 pureScale configuration and ensure that it is properly set up for use with BPM. This includes verifying that the DB2 pureScale instance is properly configured and that the BPM internal Document Store is properly connected to the DB2 pureScale instance.
2. Check the BPM configuration and ensure that it is properly set up for use with DB2 pureScale. This includes verifying that the correct database connection properties are set and that the BPM internal Document Store is properly configured.
3. If the issue persists, consider applying a fix or patch to resolve the issue. You can check the IBM BPM documentation and support websites for any known issues or fixes related to the internal Document Store and DB2 pureScale.
4. If you are still experiencing issues, contact IBM support for further assistance.

It's also recommended to check the following IBM BPM documentation for troubleshooting the internal Document Store with DB2 pureScale:

* IBM BPM documentation: Troubleshooting the internal Document Store with DB2 pureScale
* IBM BPM documentation: Configuring the internal Document Store with DB2 pureScale

Additionally, you can also use the BPM Data Collector to automatically collect basic MustGather data like logs, version info and so on. This tool can be used with IBM Business Process Manager V8.0.1 Fix Pack 3 and with IBM Business Process Manager V8.5.x.

Title: TECHQA | Q540 passage 2

MustGather; BPM document store TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You are having a BPM document store problem with the IBM Business Process Manager products. For example, you might be experiencing a problem when you attach or migrate documents. You would like to know what information you must collect (MustGather) so that the IBM Business Process Manager Support team can diagnose your problem. If you gather this documentation before contacting support it will expedite the troubleshooting process, and save you time. 

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Collect troubleshooting information for BPM document store problems in IBM Business Process Manager. Gathering this information before calling IBM support helps in understanding the problem and saves time when data is analyzed.



SETTING THE TRACE STRING

 * For issues involving adding and viewing documents, set the trace string on the AppTarget cluster to:
   ​*=info:
   WLE.*=all:
   com.ibm.bpm.embeddedecm.*=all: 
   com.ibm.bpm.integration.*=all: 
   com.ibm.bpm.auth.*=all: 
   WLE.wle_security=all: 
   WAS.clientinfopluslogging=all:
   com.lombardisoftware.component.ecmconnector.*=all 
   (Collect the trace for the entire scenario starting from document attachment to document viewing)
   
   Gather the following table exports:
   select * from LSW_BPD_INSTANCE where bpd_instance_id IN (<processinstanceid>) 
   and 
   select * from LSW_TASK where task_id in (<taskid>) 
   if the process instance ids or task ids are not available, or in the case where subprocesses or linked processes are used please send in the entire table content. 
   
   Gather the responses from the following REST API:
   <host:port>/rest/bpm/wle/v1/process/<processInstanceId>
   <host:port>/rest/bpm/wle/v1/task/<taskid> 
   
   If you are not sure which task ID or process instance ID is failing, the following lines in the above trace taken at the time of the issue should show them:
   wle 1 com.lombardisoftware.server.api.DefaultAuthorizationAPI canViewTaskInternal canViewTaskInternal(...) taskId=<taskId>) 
   and
   wle 1 com.lombardisoftware.server.api.DefaultAuthorizationAPI canViewInstance canViewInstance(...) instanceId=<processinstanceid>) 
   
   Refer to the security mustgathers for instance/task authorization:
   http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21609418 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21609418] 
 * For problems during document migration, set the trace string on the AppTarget cluster to:
   *=info:
   com.ibm.bpm.embeddedecm.*=all:
   com.lombardisoftware.server.document.migration.*=all



Note: Enabling the trace might slow down your system. This delay can lead to transaction time-outs and errors. Disable this trace after collecting the requested information. 

To set the tracing and generate a new set of logs and traces, complete the following steps for the servers or cluster that show the issue:  1. In the Integrated Solutions Console, under ​Troubleshooting​ ​>​ ​Log and Trace​, pick the server for which you want to change the settings. ​
    
    
 2. Click Change Log Detail Levels​.
    
    
 3. Select the ​configuration tab to set the tracing. This change takes effect after the next server restart and a completely new set of log files can be collected. Optionally, select the runtime​ tab to dynamically set the tracing.
    
    
 4. Enter the trace string and click OK.
    
    
 5. If you selected the configuration tab, stop the server.
    
    
 6. Archive and delete the current logs/traces/ffdc from the following directories:  * <install​​_​​root>​​/​​profiles/<profile​​_​​name>​​/​​logs​​/<​​server​​_​​name> 
     * <install​​_​​root>​​/​​profiles/<profile​​_​​name>​​/​​logs​​/ffdc
       
       
    
    
 7. Start the server and check the trace.log file to make sure that the correct tracing is set.
    
    
 8. Reproduce the problem and note down the timestamp. Check the trace.log file to ensure that the time of the issue is covered in the file. Gather the complete server log files and the ffdc directory.
    
    
 9. Delete the trace string from the Log Detail Level for your server.
    
    


Note: Ensure that you have set the Maximum trace file size (recommendation: 20MB) as well as the number of historical trace files (recommendation: 20) to an appropriate value. You can find those values at Servers > Application Servers > server_name > Diagnostic Trace Service. 

GENERAL DIAGNOSTIC INFORMATION

Collect the general troubleshooting information as described in Collect troubleshooting data for the IBM Business Process Manager products [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21569731].

In addition: 

 * Describe the problem in detail. 
 * Provide WebSphere Application Server user registry information. 
 * Ensure that the log files of the AppTarget include the server start. 
 * Run the getDocumentStoreStatus command [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmndhelp/v8r5m0/topic/com.ibm.wbpm.ref.doc/topics/rref_getdocstorestatus.html] and provide the output. 
 * Provide the FileNet logs from the <install​​_​​root>​​/​​profiles/<profile​​_​​name>​​/​​FileNet​​/<​​server​​_​​name>/p8_server_error.log andp8_server_trace.log files.


WHAT TO DO NEXT
 

 1. Review the logs and traces at the time of the problem to try to determine the source of the problem.
    
    
 2. Use IBM Support Assistant [http://www.ibm.com/software/support/isa/] to search for known problems in the information center, forums, and technotes.
    
    
 3. If you cannot find related problems or cannot solve the problem, send the information that you have collected to IBM by following the instructions in Exchanging Information with IBM Technical Support. [http://www.ibm.com/software/support/exchangeinfo.html]
    
    

RELATED INFORMATION
 Administering the IBM BPM document store [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmndhelp/v8r5m0/topic/com.ibm.wbpm.admin.doc/topics/tbpmdocadmin.html]
Migrating doc attachments to the BPM document store [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/dmndhelp/v8r5m0/topic/com.ibm.wbpm.imuc.doc/topics/tbpmdocmig_std_lin.html]
BPM cannot communicate with the BPM document store [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21673250]
The integration of IBM BPM with an ECM System generates [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21678689]
Workaround to overwrite or recreate ECM config [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1JR50446]

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
To resolve this issue, complete the following steps:


 1. Stop all of the servers or clusters.
    
    
 2. Back up the process server database for IBM Business Process Manager.
    
    
 3. Connect to process server database and execute the following SQL query: 
    select * from LSW_USR where USER_ID = '99999'
    
    
 4. Delete the row which is queried from the LSW_USR table in step 3. The row to be deleted is similar to the following row: 
    ('99999', 'RefreshLock', '1234567812345678','RefreshLock', 'T', null, null);
    
    
 5. Restart the servers or clusters one at the time.

question:
We got a NullPointerException when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT 

After logging into FileNet Workplace XT, a java.lang.NullPointerException error is logged when bootstrapping the object store or when accessing Site Preferences/Access Role.

Here's the error stack,

Error Stack Trace extracted from the J2EE Application Server error log:

java.lang.NullPointerException

at org.apache.jasper.runtime.JspWriterImpl.write(JspWriterImpl.java:421)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRoleMembers(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:254)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRole(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:220)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.renderAdminRole(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:131)

at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)
ground_truth: Based on the provided context, the NullPointerException occurring when bootstrapping the object store or accessing Site Preferences/Access Role on WorkplaceXT is likely caused by an invalid entry specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). To resolve the issue, you need to change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry, such as cn, displayName or uid, in FEM and then restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances. This should prevent the NullPointerException error from occurring.
answer: To resolve this issue, you should change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry such as cn, displayName or uid in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM) and restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances.
contexts:
Title: TECHQA | Q39 passage 0

java.lang.NullPointerException; workplacext bootstrap; user display name TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 After logging into FileNet Workplace XT, a java.lang.NullPointerException error is logged when bootstrapping the object store or when accessing Site Preferences/Access Role. 

SYMPTOM
Error Stack Trace extracted from the J2EE Application Server error log: 

java.lang.NullPointerException 

 at org.apache.jasper.runtime.JspWriterImpl.write(JspWriterImpl.java:421) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRoleMembers(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:254) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.renderRole(PrefAccessRolesInfoPage.java:220) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.renderAdminRole(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:131) 

 at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.ui.info.prefs.PrefBootstrapInfoPage.render(PrefBootstrapInfoPage.java:303)


CAUSE
This behavior may occur if an invalid entry has been specified in the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute setting in the Directory Services Configuration in FileNet Enterprise Manager (FEM). 

Workplace XT attempts to resolve the user names and groups defined within the Access Roles based on the Directory Service configuration defined in FEM. If the user name query resolves to null or if there is an invalid entry a NullPointerException error may occur. 


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 In FEM, change the User Display Name Attribute or Group Display Name Attribute to a valid LDAP entry such as cn, displayName or uid and restart the FileNet Content Engine and Workplace XT instances.

Below error displayed after logging in:

at com.ibm.ws.webcontainer.filter.WebAppFilterChain._doFilter (WebAppFilterChain.java:80)
at com.ibm.ws.webcontainer.filter.WebAppFilterManager.doFilter (WebAppFilterManager.java:908)

at com.ibm.wsspi.webcontainer.servlet.GenericServletWrapper.handleRequest (GenericServletWrapper.java:121)
at com.ibm.ws.jsp.webcontainerext.AbstractJSPExtensionServletWrapper.handleRequest (AbstractJSPExtensionServletWrapper.java:259)
at com.ibm.ws.jsp.webcontainerext.AbstractJSPExtensionProcessor.handleRequest (AbstractJSPExtensionProcessor.java:353)
at com.ibm.ws.webcontainer.webapp.WebAppRequestDispatcher.include (WebAppRequestDispatcher.java:732)
at com.filenet.wcm.toolkit.server.base.WcmController.serverSideInclude (WcmController.java:5503)

at com.ibm._jsp._WcmBootstrap._jspService (_WcmBootstrap.java:141)



CAUSE
PrimaryViews.xml file has encoding that's set incorrectly to ANSI. By default, this file should be set to UCS-2 Big Endian after XT installation.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 

 1. Open the <XT install folder>FileNet/Config/WebClient/PrimaryViews.xml into Notepad ++. 
 2. Select Encoding menu. 
 3. Select Convert to UCS-2 Big Endian then save the change. 
 4. Stop and restart the Workplace XT application server

Title: TECHQA | Q39 passage 1

nullpointer exception; workplace logon; configurationdirectory TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 FileNet Application Engine - null error message displayed instead of the logon screen. 

SYMPTOM
The following message may be displayed instead of the logon screen in FileNet Application Engine (Workplace) : 

Error Message:
null

Exception Class:
java.lang.NullPointerException

Stack Trace:
java.lang.NullPointerException 
at java.io.File. (File.java:194) 
at org.apache.log4j.helpers.FileWatchdog. (FileWatchdog.java:47) 
at org.apache.log4j.PropertyWatchdog. (PropertyConfigurator.java:665) 
at org.apache.log4j.PropertyConfigurator.configureAndWatch (PropertyConfigurator.java:373) 
at org.apache.log4j.PropertyConfigurator.configureAndWatch (PropertyConfigurator.java:355) 
at com.filenet.util.logging.LoggingConfigLoader.loadConfig (LoggingConfigLoader.java:44) 
at com.filenet.wcm.apps.server.controller.WcmWorkplaceController.initializeStaticClasses (WcmWorkplaceController.java:370) 
at com.filenet.wcm.toolkit.server.base.WcmController.configurePage (WcmController.java:2580) 
at com.filenet.wcm.toolkit.server.base.ConfigurableController.configurePage (ConfigurableController.java:115) 
at com.filenet.wcm.toolkit.server.base.WcmController.configurePage (WcmController.java:2476) 
at com.filenet.wcm.toolkit.server.base.WcmController.configurePage (WcmController.java:2339) 
at org.apache.jsp.WcmDefault_jsp._jspService (WcmDefault_jsp.java:59) ....


CAUSE
This message may be displayed when the Application Engine (Workplace) is unable to access the configuration files directory. 


DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Inspect the contents of the deployed web.xml for the Workplace application ( typically under the J2EE application server deployed directory ...\Workplace\WEB-INF\web.xml). 

The configurationDirectory parameter indicates the location of the AE configuration files directory, for example on Microsoft Windows, it may indicate : 


<context-param>
<param-name>configurationDirectory</param-name> 
<param-value>C:\\Program Files\\FileNet\\Config\\AE</param-value> 
</context-param>



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The Operating System user starting the J2EE application server needs to be able to read / write to the location identified as the "configurationDirectory" in web.xml, otherwise a NullPointerException may be displayed. Adjust file system / network share permissions as to allow access to this directory.

Title: TECHQA | Q39 passage 3

WorkplaceXT; AppInt TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Getting a NullPointerException from WorkplaceXT/Application Integration 

SYMPTOM
Getting prompted to logon twice when adding a document through Application Integration for WorkplaceXT/Microsoft Outlook and getting a NullPointerException when logging on at second time.

After the first logon, user was promoted to logon again. When logging on at the second time, the following error occurred:

--------------------------
"IBM FileNet Workplace XT Add: Unable to Perform Add Operation"

Operation: SelectedAddProfile
Error Name: E_SVRCMDXMLPARSERFAILED
HRESULT: 0x8004042b
Description: Unable to parse command XML:null
Details: java.lang.NullPointerException
-------------------------

After the error message dialog box was dismissed, adding a document from Outlook did not prompt for logon again for the current Outlook session. After starting a new Outlook session, the symptom repeated again.


CAUSE
Since the WorkplaceXT was not configured for Single Sign On (SSO), the first logon was expected for each Outlook session. The unexpected second logon was due to the Application Integration error (see above).

This error was caused by the jar files in <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory of the WebShpere Applicaton Server (WAS) hosting the WorkplaceXT.

In reference to the following page at P8 Knowledge Center 


"Configuring WebSphere Application Server to support user tokens and WebDAV connections for Workplace XT"

http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSNW2F_5.2.1/com.ibm.p8.installingxt.doc/wxtip018.htm [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSNW2F_5.2.1/com.ibm.p8.installingxt.doc/wxtip018.htm]

there should be only two jar files in the following directory in WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT:
<install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Steps to resolve the problem: 

1. Stopped WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT
2. Removed any additional jar files from the <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory so that the directory contained only two jar files: 

- authentication-websphere.jar
- log4j_1.2.13.jar

3. Deleted WorkplaceXT folder from the WAS wstemp folder, and clear the browser cache
4. Started WebSphere Application Server 

After implementing the above procedure, the problem was resolved. User started to get a logon prompt only once as expected.

question: Help with Security Bulletin: WMB and IIB are affected by 'Information Disclosure' vulnerability I need to understand details regarding Security Bulletin: WebSphere Message Broker and IBM Integration Bus are affected by 'Information Disclosure' vulnerability. Where can I find this information?
ground_truth:
You can find information regarding the 'Information Disclosure' vulnerability affecting WebSphere Message Broker and IBM Integration Bus by consulting the following security bulletin:

Security Bulletin: Information Disclosure in WebSphere Message Broker and IBM Integration Bus 
[http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22013601]
answer:
To find the details regarding the Security Bulletin: WebSphere Message Broker and IBM Integration Bus are affected by 'Information Disclosure' vulnerability, you can consult the security bulletin mentioned in the reference section of the security bulletin. 

You can access the security bulletin by visiting the provided link: http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21697107. This link will provide you with the necessary information about the vulnerability, including its details, affected products and versions, and any available fixes.

question: Is transient user support available for SAML integration with WebSphere Portal 8.0? I am looking to leverage the SAML TAI provided by WebSphere Application Server for my WebSphere Portal 8.0 environment. Is transient user support available so that I do not have to maintain a local account in my Portal user registry for every external user which is verified and trusted by my identity provider?
ground_truth: Yes, transient user support for SAML integration is available for WebSphere Portal starting with Cumulative Fix (CF) #8 for version 8.5. However, there are no plans to backport this functionality to Portal 7.0 or Portal 8.0.
answer: No, transient user support is not available for SAML integration with WebSphere Portal 8.0. According to the provided information, transient user support for SAML TAI was introduced starting with Cumulative Fix (CF) #8 for 8.5. This means it is not available for WebSphere Portal 8.0.

 ITAM TAM PD

SAML; SSO; TAI; PUMA; CWWIM1011E; InvalidUniqueNameException TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM
After configuring the Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) SAML Trust Association Interceptor (TAI) in WebSphere Application Server, you attempt to access WebSphere Portal after authenticating to your Identity Provider (IdP), but you are unable to view the Portal page.

SYMPTOM
- Portal page is blank 

- SystemOut.log contains the following error at the time of the problem occurrence:

com.ibm.websphere.wim.exception.InvalidUniqueNameException: CWWIM1011E
The '<userId>' unique name is not valid.


CAUSE
SAML TAI parameter sso_<n>.sp.idMap = is configured to use "idAssertion" which results in the userid instead of the fully distinguished name of the user to be set in the JAAS subject. Portal then uses this value to query Virtual Member Manager (VMM) via findUserByIdentifier() and the failure occurs since the value is not formatted as a distinguished name.

question:
Why do I get error when I try to save application in APMUI? I am using APMUI 7.7 IF1. Following error occurs when I try to save application:

 

CTGAZ0001E: The application cannot be saved because the application repository is not available.
messages.log of scr server shows that it is listening at 17310 port:

 

A CWWKT0016I: Web application available (default_host): http://apmhost:17310/SCRCLUSTER_SCR_oslc.html
But apmui is referring to this URL with port 8080 (APM_UI.log):

 

http://apmhost:8080/SCRCLUSTER_SCR_oslc/scr/gettime
ground_truth: The error occurs because the APMUI application is trying to save the application using the wrong port. It is attempting to access the SCRCLUSTER_SCR_oslc.html URL with port 8080 instead of the correct port 17310. This discrepancy is causing the "CTGAZ0001E: The application cannot be saved because the application repository is not available" error message to appear. To resolve this issue, you need to ensure that APMUI is configured to access the SCRCLUSTER_SCR_oslc.html URL with the correct port 17310 instead of port 8080.

question: Is transient user support available for SAML integration with WebSphere Portal 8.0? I am looking to leverage the SAML TAI provided by WebSphere Application Server for my WebSphere Portal 8.0 environment. Is transient user support available so that I do not have to maintain a local account in my Portal user registry for every external user which is verified and trusted by my identity provider?
ground_truth: Yes, transient user support for SAML integration is available for WebSphere Portal starting with Cumulative Fix (CF) #8 for version 8.5. However, there are no plans to backport this functionality to Portal 7.0 or Portal 8.0.
answer:
Transient user support is available for SAML integration with WebSphere Portal starting with Cumulative Fix (CF) #8 for 8.5. However, it is not explicitly mentioned whether this feature is available for WebSphere Portal 8.0.

If you are looking to leverage the SAML TAI provided by WebSphere Application Server for your WebSphere Portal 8.0 environment, you may want to consider using the "localRealm" value for the sso_<n>.sp.idMap property instead of "idAssertion." This will ensure that WebSphere Application Server will complete the JAAS Subject with the full distinguished name for the user by querying the user registry, thus allowing Portal to pass the correct format to VMM.

Alternatively, you may want to consider upgrading to WebSphere Portal 8.5 or later, which includes transient user support for SAML TAI.

PROBLEM
After configuring the Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) SAML Trust Association Interceptor (TAI) in WebSphere Application Server, you attempt to access WebSphere Portal after authenticating to your Identity Provider (IdP), but you are unable to view the Portal page.

SYMPTOM
- Portal page is blank 

- SystemOut.log contains the following error at the time of the problem occurrence:

com.ibm.websphere.wim.exception.InvalidUniqueNameException: CWWIM1011E
The '<userId>' unique name is not valid.


CAUSE
SAML TAI parameter sso_<n>.sp.idMap = is configured to use "idAssertion" which results in the userid instead of the fully distinguished name of the user to be set in the JAAS subject. Portal then uses this value to query Virtual Member Manager (VMM) via findUserByIdentifier() and the failure occurs since the value is not formatted as a distinguished name.


DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
- Collect Portal Login troubleshooting data [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21592791] 

- Look for the following in security.xml:
<interceptors xmi:id="TAInterceptor_<identifier>" interceptorClassName="com.ibm.ws.security.web.saml.ACSTrustAssociationInterceptor">
...
<trustProperties xmi:id="Property_<identifier>" name="sso_1.sp.idMap" value="idAssertion"/> 

Note: There may be more than one sso_<n>.sp.idMap property (for example, sso_1.sp.idMap, sso_2.sp.idMap, etc). 


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 

Option #1: 

The idAssertion value means that no entry is necessary for the user in the user registry, which is not supported in WebSphere Portal by default. Update the sso_<n>.sp.idMap property via the Integrated Solutions Console to use the value "localRealm" instead of "idAssertion." 

The localRealm value ensures that WebSphere Application Server will complete the JAAS Subject with the full distinguished name for the user by querying the user registry and thus Portal will pass the correct format to VMM. 

Option #2: 

Transient user support [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSHRKX_8.5.0/mp/overview/new_cf08.dita?lang=en] has been introduced for SAML TAI [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSHRKX_8.5.0/mp/wcm/wcm_secure_int_tai_auth.dita?lang=en] starting with Cumulative Fix (CF) #8 for 8.5. Refer to the WebSphere Portal Security blog [https://www.ibm.com/developerworks/community/blogs/8f2bc166-3bdc-4a9d-bad4-3620dbb3e46c/entry/portal_transient_user_support_with_was_saml_tai_business_case_clarification?lang=en] for more details regarding the business case and implementation details. 

 

Note: WebSphere Portal also provides transient user support when configured with OpenId authentication [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSHRKX_8.5.0/mp/security/use_openid.dita?lang=en].

security; administration; xmlaccess; password; change; spyware; malware; adware; LDAP; realm; releasebuilder; standalone; federated TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
Top Content for WebSphere Portal Security and Administration, all topics.

ANSWER
1. What steps should I follow to change Portal-related passwords?
The WebSphere Portal documentation contains instructions for both Version 6.1 [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wpdoc/v6r1/topic/com.ibm.wp.ent.doc_v615/security/sec_pswds.html] and [http://www-10.lotus.com/ldd/portalwiki.nsf/dx/Updating_user_ID_and_passwords_wp7]Version 7 [http://www-10.lotus.com/ldd/portalwiki.nsf/dx/Updating_user_ID_and_passwords_wp7].

2. How can I configure LDAP failover in WebSphere Portal?

See Implementing LDAP failover [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21382720].

3. Login fails for a single user or a small number of users and only on specific workstations. What might cause this?

Client add-ons could prevent successful login [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21441915]

4. After login a single image file is shown in the browser and nothing else, how can I resolve this?

Apply APAR PM25450 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1PM25450] then perform the manual configuration steps listed in the APAR text.

5. Should I configure the Portal for standalone or federated LDAP security?

Federated security is the out-of-the-box configuration. The configuration task wp-modify-ldap-security "converts" the configuration to standalone with a single LDAP. Use standalone if you: (1) Will not require more than one LDAP or another type user repository (for example, a database), (2) Will not need to configure multiple virtual portals, each with its own realm.

6. Is my current security configuration is standalone or federated?

Determining whether WebSphere Portal security configuration is standalone or federated [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21386736]

7. Configuration changes in our environment have caused access to Portal artifacts to be lost. How can I restore access?

See How to fix Portal Access Control settings after user/group external identifiers have changed [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21377025]

8. In our environment, updates are made to LDAP directly outside Portal. The changes are not immediately visible in the Portal or in our custom portlet. Why is this?

Both the Portal User Management Architecture (PUMA) and WebSphere Application Server Virtual Member Manager (VMM) component maintain caches that are automatically updated only if user, group or attribute changes are made using the Portal UI.

You can adjust the cache time [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21413947] out periods, disable the caches [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21379992] (this is to be avoided due to its effect on performance), or, if you have custom code that calls or can be modified to call the PUMA reload() method, apply APAR PM16430 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1PM16430] and add the associated new properties.

9. Is there a document that summarizes the ReleaseBuilder tool?

Yes. See Using ReleaseBuilder effectively [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21243005]

10. How do I collect log files or trace for a WebSphere Portal security issue?

Follow the appropriate Collecting Data [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21312740] document for the version of Portal in use.

question: Java.io.NotSerializableException in WAS We are experiencing the Java.io.NotSerializableException exception. I see this error: WTRN0047E:java.io.NotSerializableException. Anyone experienced this before?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.
answer:
Yes, I'm familiar with this issue. The Java.io.NotSerializableException in WebSphere Application Server (WAS) typically occurs when the Session Manager tries to serialize a non-serializable object. 

To resolve this issue, you need to ensure that all objects stored in the session are serializable. This means that the objects should implement the java.io.Serializable interface.

You can use the SessionInspectServlet to determine which attribute of the session object references the non-serializable object. Disabling the SessionObjectSize counter can also help to stop the error from occurring.

Here are some steps you can take to resolve the issue:

1. Identify the non-serializable object: Use the SessionInspectServlet to determine which object is causing the issue.
2. Make the object serializable: Implement the java.io.Serializable interface in the non-serializable object.
3. Test the application: Once you've made the object serializable, test the application to ensure that the issue is resolved.

If you're still experiencing issues, you can try disabling the SessionObjectSize counter to see if it stops the error from occurring.

question: Java.io.NotSerializableException in WAS We are experiencing the Java.io.NotSerializableException exception. I see this error: WTRN0047E:java.io.NotSerializableException. Anyone experienced this before?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.
answer:
The Java.io.NotSerializableException is a common issue in WebSphere Application Server (WAS) that occurs when a non-serializable object is stored in the HTTP session. This can happen when persistent sessions or PMI (Performance Monitoring Infrastructure) is enabled.

To resolve this issue, you need to ensure that all objects placed in the HTTP session implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface. This is because the session manager needs to serialize the session data into a byte array stream when PMI is enabled, and it cannot serialize non-serializable objects.

You can use the SessionInspectServlet to help you determine which attribute in the HTTP session is referencing a non-serializable object. This servlet can be accessed by navigating to the URI that you defined in your web module, and it will display the session details at that moment.

To access the SessionInspectServlet, follow these steps:

1. Define a servlet/uri entry for the SessionInspectServlet in your web module.
2. Restart your web module.
3. Open a browser and access your web module from the browser, then navigate the application in the browser to populate the session.
4. Press Ctrl+N to open a new browser, then access the SessionInspectServlet in the new browser by calling the URI that you defined in step 2.

By using the SessionInspectServlet, you can identify the non-serializable object and implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface to resolve the issue.

serializable; NotSerializableException; java.io.NotSerializableException; waseng; session; httpsession; httpsessions; persistent; ProblemDeterminationTool; ProblemDeterminationDocument TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 When Persistent sessions or PMI is enabled and a Java.io.NotSerializableException occur in logs, you will see the following example error message. 


[10/4/02 10:11:10:172 EDT] 28b7c18b SessionContex X WTRN0047E:
java.io.NotSerializableException: COM.ibm.db2.jdbc.app.DB2Connection



CAUSE
The class name at the end of the exception is the object that does not implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface. In most instances, this object is the attribute that was put into the session object; however, sometimes this object is referenced by an attribute of the session object. 

 To determine which attribute of the session object references this non-serializable object, access the SessionInspectServlet.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Implement the Java.io.Serializable or Java.io.Externalizable interface

With persistent sessions enabled, any object that is put into the HTTPSession must implement the serializable interface in order for session manager to store the session data outside of JVM.

When PMI is enabled, there is a counter for session data size. In order to evaluate this counter, the session data must be serialized into a byte array stream. Any object that is put into the HTTPSession must implement the serializable interface in order for session manager to convert the session data objects into byte array stream.

For example:

public classMyObject implementsSerializable{
}//end of class MyObject

Check that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface.


 1. Acess the SessionInspectServlet
    
    If the class name printed in the exception is not an object that is explicitly placed into the HTTPSession, use the SessionInspectServlet to determine the attribute in the HTTPSession that references a non-serializable object. 



SessionInspectServlet helps application writers to determine if application is written to support session persistence. SessionInspectServlet helps to: 

 * Determine attributes present in the session
   
 * Determine serializable and non-serializable attributes in the session
   
 * Determine the size of each serializable attribute in session
   
 * Determine if serializable attribute is implemented properly (if serializable attribute and all its internals are serializable)


Servlet serializes and deserializes attributes into memory to simulate session persistence; therefore, application writers need not turn on session persistence to run this servlet. However, you can run it with persistent sessions enabled. 

How to Access the SessionInspectServlet: 
 1. com.ibm.ws.webcontainer.httpsession.SessionInspectServlet is shipped with WebSphere Application Server. It is already in the classpath and does not need to be added to the application. 
    
    Define a servlet/uri entry for class SessionInspectServlet. If ServeServletsByClassnameEnabled is set to true (check the ibm-web-ext.xmi file in the WEB-INF directory of your webModule) in your webModule, servlet/uri definition is not needed. 
    
 2. Restart your Web module.
    
 3. Open a browser and access your Web module from the browser, then navigate the application in the browser to populate the session.
    
 4. Press Crtl+N to open a new browser, then access the SessionInspectServlet in the new browser by calling the URI that you defined in step 2. If you are using ServeServletsbyClassname, the URI will be "webapp_context_root\servlet\com.ibm.ws.webcontainer.httpsession.SessionInspectServlet". This displays the session details at that moment. You can access the servlet as you navigate your application to determine the session contents as you go along. Note: The servlet does not support attributes in the session that are outside the WebSphere Application Server scope. That is, session contents need to be accessed by the server class loader scope.

RAD; EAR; WAS; publish; start; server; ejb-jar.xml; DeploymentDescriptorLoadException; IllegalStateException; EMF2DOMAdapterImpl_ERROR_0; webpshere; runtime; jars; com.ibm.ws.runtime.jar TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Publishing an EAR in IBM Rational Application Developer (RAD) v8.0.4.2-InterimFix001 on WebSphere Application Server (WAS) v7.0.0.25 fails consistently with "DeploymentDescriptorLoadException: META-INF/ejb-jar.xml and nested exception: java.lang.IllegalStateException: EMF2DOMAdapterImpl_ERROR_0" 

SYMPTOM
Publishing in RAD v8.0.4.2-Fix001 on WAS v7.0.0.25 fails consistently with the following error in the RAD server console and SystemOut.log:

[5/13/13 17:39:41:128 EDT] 000000ad SystemErr R org.eclipse.jst.
j2ee.commonarchivecore.internal.exception.
DeploymentDescriptorLoadException: META-INF/ejb-jar.xml
Stack trace of nested exception:
java.lang.IllegalStateException: EMF2DOMAdapterImpl_ERROR_0

The EAR archive does not contain any modules with ejb-jar.xml.
Publishing directly through WAS admin console avoids the error.

Another scenario used WAS v8 EJB Embeddable Container (EJB 3.1) in the RAD v8.0 IDE to do JUnit testing on a Stateless Session Bean in an EJB 3.0 module/jar without an ejb-jar.xml deployment descriptor. The JUnit tries to start but failed. 

Encountered EJBException at runtime: 

javax.ejb.EJBException: Failed to initialize embeddable EJB container; 
nested exception is: org.eclipse.jst.j2ee.commonarchivecore.internal. 
exception.DeploymentDescriptorLoadException: META-INF/ejb-jar.xml 
org.eclipse.jst.j2ee.commonarchivecore.internal.exception. 
DeploymentDescriptorLoadException: META-INF/ejb-jar.xml 
Stack trace of nested exception: 
java.lang.IllegalStateException: EMF2DOMAdapterImpl_ERROR_0 

at org.eclipse.wst.common.internal.emf.resource.EMF2DOMAdapterImpl. 
handleInvalidMultiNodes(EMF2DOMAdapterImpl.java:1381) 
at ...


CAUSE
The EAR project/module had a WebSphere com.ibm.ws.runtime.jar with a malformed ejb-jar.xml as a utility jar. It was on the runtime classpath of other modules in the EAR.

ENVIRONMENT
Windows 7 Professional.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Search for a com.ibm.ws.runtime.jar on the runtime classpath.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The project/module included the WAS com.ibm.ws.runtime.jar on the classpath. For some unknown reason, this JAR contained a malformed ejb-jar.xml (deployment descriptor). This jar had to be removed JAR from the classpath to resolve the problem. In general, WebSphere runtime jars should not be in the EAR to be used on a member module's runtime classpath, as they are already provided in the server runtime and typically loaded as needed by the server. Such jars could also conflict with up to date similar jar(s) in the server runtime.





As an aside, the java.class.path was very large and contained many unnecessary JARs which were removed. If this specific solution does not apply in your case, contact IBM Support for Rational Application Developer. 

The Related Information section has items showing similar WebSphere runtime error message scenarios involving other deployment descriptors.

question: Java.io.NotSerializableException in WAS We are experiencing the Java.io.NotSerializableException exception. I see this error: WTRN0047E:java.io.NotSerializableException. Anyone experienced this before?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.
answer:
To resolve the Java.io.NotSerializableException exception in WAS, follow these steps:

1. Check the SystemOut.log file for the exact error message, which should indicate the class name of the non-serializable object.
2. Use the SessionInspectServlet to determine the attributes in the session that reference the non-serializable object.
3. Identify the object that needs to implement the java.io.Serializable interface.
4. Modify the object to implement the java.io.Serializable interface by adding the implements Serializable clause to the class definition and implementing the writeObject and readObject methods.
5. Restart the application and verify that the Java.io.NotSerializableException exception is resolved.

If the issue persists, you may need to investigate further to determine the root cause of the problem.

Title: TECHQA | Q409 passage 4

TRIINSTALL; TRIDATABASE; TRICONFIG; TRILICENSE; TRIUI TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 License Expired error message 

SYMPTOM
Attempts to login to the application causes an error message to display indicating that the license has expired. The error messages that will appear in the WAS SystemOut.log file are: 

DSRA8201W: DataSource Configuration: DSRA8040I: Failed to connect to the DataSource. Encountered java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01017: invalid username/password; logon denied 

DSRA001E: SQL State = 7200. Error Code = 1,1017. java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01017: invalid username/password; logon denied


CAUSE
There are two reasons why this error can appear:
1 - The license has truly expired.
2 - Connectivity to the database has failed so the ability to validate the license fails causing the error message.


ENVIRONMENT
Database server
Application/Process servers



DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
The server.log file for either the application and/or process servers will indicate an issue connecting to the database. 

Example error message found in the server.log file or WAS SystemOut log:

DSRA8201W: DataSource Configuration: DSRA8040I: Failed to connect to the DataSource. Encountered java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01017: invalid username/password; logon denied
DSRA0010E: SQL State = 72000, Error Code = 1,017.
java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01017: invalid username/password; logon denied


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Clients should first validate that the database is running and that connections from the application/process servers to the database work. If access is done via a web server, verify that the web server has access to the database server. Check to see if there are any firewall rules or proxy servers in the network configuration that may be preventing the connection to the DB server.

question: Why does my JVMSERVER remain in a "BEING DISABLED"  state? When I try to disable a JVMSERVER it becomes hung and remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state. This also occurs when I shut down the CICS region. The CICS region ultimately has to be cancelled.
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the issue with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state could be due to the zFS files not being properly propagated to the target environments after applying preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS. When the updated zFS files are not rolled out to the target environments, it can impact the functioning of the JVM Servers.

To resolve this issue, ensure that the updated zFS files are properly propagated to all target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS. This should allow the JVMSERVER to be disabled successfully without getting stuck in a "BEING DISABLED" state.
answer:
Based on the provided context, it appears that the issue you're experiencing with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state is related to the Language Environment Enclave not being created. This is indicated by the error message DFHSJ0914.

The cause of this issue is likely due to zFS files not being propagated to the target environment(s). This is mentioned in the "CAUSE" section of the problem description.

To resolve this issue, you should ensure that the zFS files are properly propagated to the target environment(s). This should allow the Language Environment Enclave to be created, and the JVMSERVER should no longer remain in a "BEING DISABLED" state.

Additionally, the problem description mentions a separate issue related to threads hanging due to a deadlock when stopping an application that includes a resource adapter. However, this appears to be a separate problem from the one you're experiencing with the JVMSERVER.

It's worth noting that the problem description also mentions a workaround for the separate issue, which involves setting the property `sib.msgstore.jdbcFailoverOnDBConnectionLoss` to `false`. However, this is not directly related to the issue you're experiencing with the JVMSERVER.

 You install preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS). Now when you install a JVMServer you receive DFHSJ0214 and DFHSJ0914 error messages. 

SYMPTOM
DFHSJ0215 [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v5r1/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/DFHSJ/DFHSJ0215.html] The JVM Server jvmserver failed to initialize the OSGi framework. The JVM will be terminated. 

DFHSJ0914 [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v5r1/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/DFHSJ/DFHSJ0914E.html] JVMSERVER jvmserver is DISABLED because the Language Environment Enclave was not created.


CAUSE
zFS files not propagated to target environment(s)

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  An application server JVM can hang if a thread logging an FFDC
   hangs.
   
   The problem can be detected by observing the WSVR0605W hung
   thread notifications in the SystemOut.log.
   
   One thread will be running the FFDCFilter.processException()
   which can be invoked from any WebSphere Application Server
   component.  The thread will get stuck doing its work.
   In the following example, the thread which invoked
   FFDCFilter.processException() is stuck waiting on a response to
   the OracleConnectionWrapper.getMetaData() method call:
   
   [05/24/12 12:06:35:286 EST] 00000016 ThreadMonitor W
   WSVR0605W: Thread "ORB.thread.pool : 32" (000000d6) has
   been active for 304721 milliseconds and may be hung.  There
   is/are 16 thread(s) in total in the server that may be hung.
    at
   oracle.jdbc.OracleConnectionWrapper.getMetaData(OracleConnection
   Wrapper.java:150)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.spi.WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl.introspectSe

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   * USERS AFFECTED:  All users of IBM WebSphere Application      *
   *                  Server with applications that include a     *
   *                  resource adapter.                           *
   ****************************************************************
   * PROBLEM DESCRIPTION: Threads hang due to a deadlock when     *
   *                      stopping an application that includes   *
   *                      a resource adapter.                     *
   ****************************************************************
   * RECOMMENDATION:                                              *
   ****************************************************************
   A deadlock can occur between the application stop thread and
   the j2c resource adapter work thread doing a lookup due to
   synchronization and issues which can prevent stopping the
   application.

If this behavior is not needed and portal JVM needs to be up and running, then one can set the property, sib.msgstore.jdbcFailoverOnDBConnectionLoss to false which will cause the messaging engine to retry the connection instead of terminating the jvm

question: Why does my JVMSERVER remain in a "BEING DISABLED"  state? When I try to disable a JVMSERVER it becomes hung and remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state. This also occurs when I shut down the CICS region. The CICS region ultimately has to be cancelled.
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the issue with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state could be due to the zFS files not being properly propagated to the target environments after applying preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS. When the updated zFS files are not rolled out to the target environments, it can impact the functioning of the JVM Servers.

To resolve this issue, ensure that the updated zFS files are properly propagated to all target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS. This should allow the JVMSERVER to be disabled successfully without getting stuck in a "BEING DISABLED" state.
answer: The JVMSERVER remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state because the Language Environment Enclave was not created. This is likely due to a procedural error where the updated zFS files were not rolled out to the target environments after applying maintenance to CICS. As a result, the necessary files for the Language Environment Enclave are not available, causing the JVMSERVER to become hung and remain in a "BEING DISABLED" state.

CAUSE
zFS files not propagated to target environment(s)


DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
When you applied the maintenance, the zFS files were updated. However, due to a procedural error, these updated files were not rolled out to the target environments. Now that you are running JVM Servers, this has more of an impact.



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Ensure that the updated zFS files are propagated to the target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS.

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You receive message CWPZI8840E Failed to enable CICS JVMSERVER jvm server when trying to use the Feature Pack for Dynamic Scripting V1.0 in CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V4.2. When you enter a zero command, you also receive message CWPZI8735E Request failed. ZEROCICS return code=0, ZEROSERV return code=203 (RC=0 RC0 RC=203 RC203). 

SYMPTOM
These are the error message that display on the terminal when you try to enter a zero command such as zero version or zero help from UNIX System Services (USS):
CWPZI8735E: Request failed. ZEROCICS return code=0, ZEROSERV return code=203. 
CWPZI8840E: Failed to enable CICS JVMSERVER jvmserver. initializing the cli failed

These are the error messages in CICS region log:
DFHSJ0210 date time applid An attempt to start a JVM for the JVMSERVER resource jvmserver has failed. Reason code: JVMPROFILE_ERROR.
DFHSJ0914 E date time applid userid JVMSERVER jvmserver is DISABLED because the Language Environment Enclave was not created.
DFHSJ0912 date time applid userid JVMSERVER jvmserver was successfully discarded. Reason code:JVMPROFILE_ERROR.
date time GMT *Exc*: Problem encountered on line 50 of the JVM profile jvmserver = LIBPATH and JAVA_HOME are both specified. They are mutually exclusive.


CAUSE
The CICS TS Feature Pack for Dynamic Scripting V1.0 is for CICS TS V4.1 and is not compatible with CICS TS V4.2. This is because this feature pack has environment variables set for using the 31bit JVM and CICS TS 4.2 only uses a 64bit JVM.

Note: The key failure is that JVM server can not be started due to the incompatibility between the 31bit and 64bit JVM. This failure happens regardless of the JVM profile error (LIBPATH and JAVA_HOME are both specified).

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Use the Feature Pack for Dynamic Scripting V1.1 available for CICS TS 4.2 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21566483].

Do *NOT* attempt to deploy the existing Dynamic Scripting Feature Pack for CICS TS V4.1 into your CICS TS V4.2 environment.

RELATED INFORMATION
#CICS TS Feature Pack for Dynamic Scripting [http://www.ibm.com/software/htp/cics/scripting/]

 CICS/TS CICS TS CICS Transaction Server
